_title: BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 Appendix 3 — Time/Current Characteristics of Overcurrent Protective Devices
_note: Tripping time depends on the multiple of In flowing through the device. These curves determine selectivity coordination and disconnection time compliance. Manufacturer's curves should be used for final design — these are typical envelope values.

thermal_region.description: Low overload (1.13× to 5× In typically). Bimetallic strip heats slowly. Trip time decreases with current (inverse-time characteristic).
thermal_region.purpose: Trips on sustained overload (cable overheating risk).

magnetic_region.description: High overload / short-circuit (above the instantaneous threshold Ia). Solenoid trips almost instantly.
magnetic_region.purpose: Trips on short-circuit (cable damage and switchgear stress).

characteristic_explanation.transition: Between thermal and magnetic, there is a 'dead band' where the device may take 0.1 to 1 second to operate. Selectivity studies must account for this.

type_B_MCB_BS_EN_60898._reference: BS EN 60898 Type B characteristic, instantaneous trip 3-5× In
type_B_MCB_BS_EN_60898._use: Resistive loads, general lighting, sockets — minimal inrush
type_B_MCB_BS_EN_60898.instantaneous_trip_threshold_Ia: 3 × In to 5 × In (BS uses 5× for worst-case Zs)

type_C_MCB_BS_EN_60898._reference: BS EN 60898 Type C characteristic, instantaneous trip 5-10× In
type_C_MCB_BS_EN_60898._use: Inductive loads, motor circuits, LED panel banks with significant inrush, fluorescent lighting (older)
type_C_MCB_BS_EN_60898.instantaneous_trip_threshold_Ia: 5 × In to 10 × In (BS uses 10× for worst-case Zs)

type_D_MCB_BS_EN_60898._reference: BS EN 60898 Type D characteristic, instantaneous trip 10-20× In
type_D_MCB_BS_EN_60898._use: Transformer primary, lift drives, large discharge lighting, welding sets — very high inrush
type_D_MCB_BS_EN_60898.instantaneous_trip_threshold_Ia: 10 × In to 20 × In (BS uses 20× for worst-case Zs)

BS_88_3_fuse_gG._reference: BS 88-3 — Low-voltage fuses, gG (general application) class
BS_88_3_fuse_gG._use: Submains, distribution boards, motor circuits (with overload relay)
BS_88_3_fuse_gG._characteristic: Steeper than MCBs at high overload — trips faster at high faults. Slower than MCBs at moderate overload.

RCBO_BS_EN_61009._note: Same overcurrent characteristic as MCB of equivalent type. Plus RCD function.

RCD_trip_times_required._basis: Trip time AT 100% IΔn and AT 5× IΔn
RCD_trip_times_required.S_type_time_delayed_at_IΔn: Between 130 ms and 500 ms (intentional delay for selectivity)

current_discrimination.principle: Downstream device clears fault before upstream device starts to operate
current_discrimination.rule_of_thumb: Upstream In ≥ 1.6 × downstream In to achieve discrimination at moderate overload
current_discrimination.limitation: Above the upstream device's Ia, both will see instantaneous trip — discrimination lost. Use time grading or energy discrimination above this.

time_grading.principle: Upstream device intentionally delayed so downstream device trips first even on high fault
time_grading.implementation: S-type RCDs (40-130 ms delay), MCCBs with short-time delay (S-band), ACBs with adjustable LSIG settings
time_grading.rule_of_thumb: Minimum 100 ms between cascaded device steps

energy_discrimination.principle: Downstream device dissipates fault energy (∫I²dt) before upstream device reaches its trip threshold
energy_discrimination.implementation: Use manufacturer-published I²t coordination tables — only valid within ONE manufacturer's range
energy_discrimination.limitation: Not transferable across brands. Requires detailed data not in generic curves.

zone_selective_interlocking_ZSI.principle: Electronic communication between cascaded breakers — downstream sees fault, signals upstream, upstream delays trip until downstream clears
zone_selective_interlocking_ZSI.implementation: Available on ACBs and high-end MCCBs. Requires control wiring or fiber link between devices.
zone_selective_interlocking_ZSI.advantage: Achieves selectivity AND fast clearance simultaneously — overcomes the speed/selectivity tradeoff of time grading.

let_through_energy_I2t._purpose: For adiabatic check (Reg 434.5.2) and for fault discrimination — how much I²·t the device allows through before clearing
let_through_energy_I2t.MCB_typical_I2t_at_10kA_RMS_3kA_let_thro: Manufacturer specific — typical 6kA MCB lets through approximately 5,000 A²s
let_through_energy_I2t.fuse_typical_I2t_pre_arc_kA2s: Steeply lower than MCB — gG fuse lets through approximately 1,000-3,000 A²s at high currents
let_through_energy_I2t.implication: For high-fault circuits with small cables, fuses often give better cable protection than MCBs.

domestic_consumer_unit.final_circuit: 32A Type B MCB on ring main
domestic_consumer_unit.selectivity_verdict: Type B MCB discriminates with 100A fuse up to approximately 600A fault. Above that, both will trip simultaneously — acceptable for domestic since whole-house disconnection is not catastrophic.

commercial_office.incoming: 400A MCCB at intake, electronic trip, LSIG settings
commercial_office.selectivity_verdict: Setup correctly with short-time delay on intake MCCB (50ms delay) and 100A MCCB selected for discrimination, RCBO trips first on most faults. Major fault (>4kA at RCBO) may cause both RCBO and 100A MCCB to trip — acceptable since intake (400A) remains intact.

industrial_MV_LV.MCC_starter: Local motor starter fuse 80A + overload relay
industrial_MV_LV.selectivity_verdict: ZSI ensures only the nearest device to the fault trips. Even on heavy short-circuit at the motor, only the 80A starter operates — the rest of the plant continues running.

limitations_of_this_data._note: Values in this file are TYPICAL envelope values for the device type. For actual design:
limitations_of_this_data.use_manufacturer_data: Always use the manufacturer's published time/current curves for the specific product being installed. Curves vary between manufacturers and between product ranges within a manufacturer.
limitations_of_this_data.tolerance_band: Real devices have a tolerance band — typical curves represent the centre. Worst-case selectivity requires considering the upstream device at its FASTEST tolerance and the downstream device at its SLOWEST tolerance.
limitations_of_this_data.temperature_dependence: Thermal trip characteristics shift with ambient temperature — manufacturer's data typically published at 30°C. Adjust for actual ambient if at extremes.
limitations_of_this_data.ageing: Trip characteristics drift slowly with age — annual functional testing detects significant drift.
